result.stdout:
Description:
  A cli tool helps you to de-duplicate images in a folder.

Usage:
  nibot [command] [options]

Options:
  -v, --verbose   Show detailed log
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  dedup        De-duplicate images in a folder.
  dedup-copy   Copy images in a folder to another folder with de-duplication. (Duplicate images in destination folder will be ignored.)
  dedup-patch  Dedup patch will fetch all files from source directory and destination directory, and if a picture in source directory is a duplicate of a picture in destination directory, and source picture has a higher quality, then the source picture will be patched to the destination picture.
  compare      Compare two images to see their similarity.
  dup-top      Find top similar images in a folder compared to a source image.

commands.payload:
Description:
  A cli tool helps you to de-duplicate images in a folder.

Usage:
  nibot [command] [options]

Options:
  -v, --verbose   Show detailed log
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  dedup        De-duplicate images in a folder.
  dedup-copy   Copy images in a folder to another folder with de-duplication. (Duplicate images in destination folder will be ignored.)
  dedup-patch  Dedup patch will fetch all files from source directory and destination directory, and if a picture in source directory is a duplicate of a picture in destination directory, and source picture has a higher quality, then the source picture will be patched to the destination picture.
  compare      Compare two images to see their similarity.
  dup-top      Find top similar images in a folder compared to a source image.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Compare two images to see their similarity.

Usage:
  nibot compare [options]

Options:
  -i, --images <images> (REQUIRED)  Paths of the images to compare. You can pass this '-i' parameter multiple times to compare at least two images.
  -?, -h, --help                    Show help and usage information
  -v, --verbose                     Show detailed log

commands.payload:
Description:
  Compare two images to see their similarity.

Usage:
  nibot compare [options]

Options:
  -i, --images <images> (REQUIRED)  Paths of the images to compare. You can pass this '-i' parameter multiple times to compare at least two images.
  -?, -h, --help                    Show help and usage information
  -v, --verbose                     Show detailed log

result.stdout:
Description:
  De-duplicate images in a folder.

Usage:
  nibot dedup [options]

Options:
  -p, --path <path> (REQUIRED)                                                            Path of the folder to de-duplicate. (Symbolic links will be ignored)
  -ds, --duplicate-similar <duplicate-similar>                                            Similarity bar. This value means two image are considered as duplicates if their similarity is greater than it. Setting too small may cause different images to be considered as duplicates. Suggested values: [96-100] [default: 96]
  -r, --recursive                                                                         Recursively search for similar images in subdirectories. (.trash content will be ignored)
  -k, --keep <keep>                                                                       Preference for sorting images by quality to determine which to keep when duplicates are found. Available options: Colorful|GrayScale|Newest|Oldest|Smallest|Largest|HighestResolution|LowestResolution. [default: Colorful|HighestResolution|Largest|Newest]
  -a, --action <Delete|DeleteAndCreateLink|MoveToTrash|MoveToTrashAndCreateLink|Nothing>  Action to take when duplicates are found. Available options: Nothing, Delete, MoveToTrash. [default: MoveToTrash]
  -y, --yes                                                                               No interactive mode. Taking action without asking for confirmation.
  -e, --extensions <extensions>                                                           Extensions of files to dedup. [default: jpg|jpeg|png|jfif]
  -t, --threads <threads>                                                                 Number of threads to use for image indexing. Default is 4. [default: 4]
  -?, -h, --help                                                                          Show help and usage information
  -v, --verbose                                                                           Show detailed log

Options:

result.stdout:
Description:
  Copy images in a folder to another folder with de-duplication. (Duplicate images in destination folder will be ignored.)

Usage:
  nibot dedup-copy [options]

Options:
  -s, --source <source> (REQUIRED)              Path of the folder to de-duplicate. (Symbolic links will be ignored)
  -d, --destination <destination> (REQUIRED)    Path of the folder to copy de-duplicated images.
  -ds, --duplicate-similar <duplicate-similar>  Similarity bar. This value means two image are considered as duplicates if their similarity is greater than it. Setting too small may cause different images to be considered as duplicates. Suggested values: [96-100] [default: 96]
  -r, --recursive                               Recursively search for similar images in subdirectories. (.trash content will be ignored)
  -k, --keep <keep>                             Preference for sorting images by quality to determine which to keep when duplicates are found. Available options: Colorful|GrayScale|Newest|Oldest|Smallest|Largest|HighestResolution|LowestResolution. [default: Colorful|HighestResolution|Largest|Newest]
  -y, --yes                                     No interactive mode. Taking action without asking for confirmation.
  -e, --extensions <extensions>                 Extensions of files to dedup. [default: jpg|jpeg|png|jfif]
  -t, --threads <threads>                       Number of threads to use for image indexing. Default is 4. [default: 4]
  -?, -h, --help                                Show help and usage information
  -v, --verbose                                 Show detailed log

Description:
  Dedup patch will fetch all files from source directory and destination directory, and if a picture in source directory is a duplicate of a picture in destination directory, and source picture has a higher quality, then the source picture will be patched to the destination picture.

Usage:
  nibot dedup-patch [options]

Options:
  -s, --source <source> (REQUIRED)              Path of the folder to de-duplicate. (Symbolic links will be ignored)
  -d, --destination <destination> (REQUIRED)    Path of the folder to copy de-duplicated images.
  -ds, --duplicate-similar <duplicate-similar>  Similarity bar. This value means two image are considered as duplicates if their similarity is greater than it. Setting too small may cause different images to be considered as duplicates. Suggested values: [96-100] [default: 96]
  -r, --recursive                               Recursively search for similar images in subdirectories. (.trash content will be ignored)
  -k, --keep <keep>                             Preference for sorting images by quality to determine which to keep when duplicates are found. Available options: Colorful|GrayScale|Newest|Oldest|Smallest|Largest|HighestResolution|LowestResolution. [default: Colorful|HighestResolution|Largest|Newest]
  -e, --extensions <extensions>                 Extensions of files to dedup. [default: jpg|jpeg|png|jfif]
  -t, --threads <threads>                       Number of threads to use for image indexing. Default is 4. [default: 4]
  -?, -h, --help                                Show help and usage information
  -v, --verbose                                 Show detailed log

result.stdout:
Description:
  Find top similar images in a folder compared to a source image.

Usage:
  nibot dup-top [options]

Options:
  -i, --input <input> (REQUIRED)    Path to the source image for comparison.
  -s, --source <source> (REQUIRED)  Path of the folder to de-duplicate. (Symbolic links will be ignored)
  -r, --recursive                   Recursively search for similar images in subdirectories. (.trash content will be ignored)
  -e, --extensions <extensions>     Extensions of files to dedup. [default: jpg|jpeg|png|jfif]
  -t, --threads <threads>           Number of threads to use for image indexing. Default is 4. [default: 4]
  --top <top>                       Number of top similar images to return. [default: 15]
  -?, -h, --help                    Show help and usage information
  -v, --verbose                     Show detailed log

commands.payload:
Description:
  Find top similar images in a folder compared to a source image.

Usage:
  nibot dup-top [options]

Options:
  -i, --input <input> (REQUIRED)    Path to the source image for comparison.
  -s, --source <source> (REQUIRED)  Path of the folder to de-duplicate. (Symbolic links will be ignored)
  -r, --recursive                   Recursively search for similar images in subdirectories. (.trash content will be ignored)
  -e, --extensions <extensions>     Extensions of files to dedup. [default: jpg|jpeg|png|jfif]
  -t, --threads <threads>           Number of threads to use for image indexing. Default is 4. [default: 4]
  --top <top>                       Number of top similar images to return. [default: 15]
  -?, -h, --help                    Show help and usage information
  -v, --verbose                     Show detailed log
